title: tui/app.rs: hpke_identity_secret stored as plain [u8;32], not Zeroizing
description: tui/src/app.rs:109 and tui/src/main.rs:124-131 — AppState.hpke_identity_secret is [u8;32], not Zeroizing<[u8;32]>. The field comment says 'KEY MATERIAL — never log' but the type does not guarantee zeroing on drop. Identity::hpke_secret_bytes() also returns [u8;32] (tracked as nie-kef6.103). Consequence: the X25519 secret key lives in heap memory for the session duration and may end up in a core dump or swap. Same issue as room_hpke_secret, which IS Zeroizing — this inconsistency means one survives drop and the other does not. Fix: change hpke_identity_secret to Zeroizing<[u8;32]>.
